item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: As image is owned by author, image is released under CC BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hehe… its a gum tree. Get it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I confess I don't know what the GOI is, but the Anomalous Object has something to do with gum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any hints as to what Anomalous Object-4805 and GOI-984 are?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The point of being vague was to let the reader think about what they thought happened for themselves. Also, the randomness is the whole point of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted for that [DATA EXPUNGED] crap to do with the object coming from space.
Is there a conceivable point to the expungement other than to fuck with people? Not that I can tell.
Are there hints as to what's behind that giant cockblock label? Not that I can see.
Is it the tree falling from space? That would be an interesting image, and mysterious. But the letter at the end seems to indicate otherwise. So we have a big, amorphous nothing falling from space.
That adds a lot, and by that I mean it adds precisely fuck nothing and why even have it in the bloody article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The randomness is the whole point of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: accidental double post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find the randomness in this article without any particular hook, and seemingly without rhyme and reason. Sorry - didn't got me. Also Ethyl Merman (?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am deeply disappointed that this is not titled "The Balloon Tree". =|

+1 anyway, because my stubborn fondness of children's books I don't fully remember is no reason to not upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. Crazy, strange, and confusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this, but was unclear why Ethel Merman is redacted. Not sure what that redaction adds to it, especially if she's a posthumous POI—seems like it would be reasonable to include her name, especially after death. Just doesn't past ye olde "this is a reference document, and info that is helpful should be in it" test. Didn't stop me from upvoting, but it does stand out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed. Glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I especially like the administrative-level what-the-fuck going on. Like, showing us how the Foundation handles things so incredibly weird and nonsensical, instead of the usual "here it is."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot. +1.

(Nitpick: Neither "mesopause" nor "sugar maple tree" are proper nouns. Fixed that for you.)

e: Oh, two other things.

SCP-2033 is a sugar maple tree (Acer saccharum) measuring approximately 3m meters tall, with its trunk measuring approximately 0.2m in width.

1. Foresters use a measure called "diameter at breast height", or DBH, to describe tree-trunk sizes: it's the diameter at about 1.5 meters off the ground (chest height or thereabouts). Even if that's too jargony for your taste, though, I'd strongly recommend changing "width" to "diameter" — it's ambiguous as it is.

2. "3m meters"? For one thing, 3m is extraordinarily short for a sugar maple with a 20cm DBH. (I'd expect it to be 10m or so.) For another, you've effectively got "meters" in there twice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's weird, it's strange, you'd sound like an idiot if you describe it to your friend. This is an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for the sheer what the fuck factor. Like that you managed to get a good story in there too.

In other news, stupid people in my Chem class started a fire. It's out now, but it had the potential to blow up. I think I'll take my leave for now and get to the rest of the new SCiPs when I'm not likely to get killed by stupid people with fire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in chat, like it here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not particularly enthusiastic about the execution, but a tree that pops singing gum bubbles is just too silly to be downvoted (plus, I tend to like SCPs that don't threaten to destroy the universe if you turn their back to them).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A tree, that might be alien in origin, that likes to make bubbles that, when they pop, sing Ethel Merman songs.

That's nuts. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is an "Y" left. Plus I think it is "Anomalous Items", not "Objects".

E: And it is "extraterrestrial". You wrote the tag wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember seeing this in chat several thousand times.

I'm glad that the random tree is finally here to stay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember seeing this in chat once; I recognize the image.

The notes in the first addendum don't do anything for me, and you've got an SCP-Y-1 in there still. Also the expungement seems rather ineffective.

However, overall I like it, it's quirky enough for an upvote.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
And thus, the weirdness of this article has gone live.

The picture was taken by me, as opposed to being taken from the web.
